_publications._publicationTitle: ALTERED CALCIUM CURRENTS AND AXONAL GROWTH IN Nf1 HAPLOINSUFFICIENT MICE.

_contextSnippet: V50% was −7.7 ± 1.1 mV (n=9) for wild-type neurons, which was significantly different from that of wild-type neurons (−16.3± 1.9 mV, n=7; p <0.05, Student's t-test)
details: N-type calcium currents showed hyperpolarizing shift in activation: V50% was -16.3± 1.9 mV in Nf1+/- versus -7.7 ± 1.1 mV in wild-type (p<0.05)
